input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule F.8.5.3 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.8.5.3
            TITLE: if the impact attenuator is attached by bonding:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.8.5.3: If the Impact Attenuator is attached by bonding:
F.8.5.3 If the Impact Attenuator is attached by bonding:  
a. Bonding must meet F.5.5 
b. The shear strength of the bond must be higher than: 
• 95 kN for foam Impact Attenuators 
• 38.5 kN for honeycomb Impact Attenuators 
• The maximum compressive force for custom Impact Attenuators 
c. The entire surface of a foam Impact Attenuator must be bonded  
d. Only the pre-crushed area of a honeycomb Impact Attenuator may be used for bond 
equivalence

final_answer: rule F.8.5.3: If the Impact Attenuator is attached by bonding: F.8.5.3 If the Impact Attenuator is attached by bonding: a. Bonding must meet F.5.5 b. The shear strength of the bond must be higher than: • 95 kN for foam Impact Attenuators • 38.5 kN for honeycomb Impact Attenuators • The maximum compressive force for custom Impact Attenuators c. The entire surface of a foam Impact Attenuator must be bonded d. Only the pre-crushed area of a honeycomb Impact Attenuator may be used for bond equivalence
